seoTitle: Gungahlin vs Hackett — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gungahlin and Hackett. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gungahlin and Hackett.
heading: Gungahlin vs Hackett — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gungahlin, Australian Capital Territory and Hackett, Australian Capital Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gungahlin has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Hackett (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gungahlin or Hackett safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gungahlin has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Hackett scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gungahlin scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Hackett scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gungahlin or Hackett?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gungahlin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
